message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): invalid-URL inputs return Err without panic (tick 343)

Added `distributed_http_client_rejects_invalid_urls_without_panic` —
pins that `DistributedHttpClient::get` returns `Err` cleanly (no
panic, bounded <5s latency) on five malformed inputs:

- `""` (empty string)
- `"not-a-url"` (no scheme)
- `"ftp://example.com/"` (unsupported scheme)
- `"http://"` (scheme-only, no authority)
- `"://bad"` (missing scheme before colon)

Why this matters: the distributed HTTP client is invoked from runtime
paths where URLs may be operator-supplied (config) or peer-supplied
(discovery/introspection). A panic on bad input would crash the
containing task; a hang would stall an agent-mediated workflow. Both
are production risks. The contract is graceful refusal.

Intentionally does not assert the specific error variant — URL
parsing is a library-owned detail (asupersync::http::h1::http_client)
that may classify each input differently. The stable observable
contract is three properties per input:
1. Result is Err
2. Bounded latency (<5s)
3. No panic

A single test iterates all five inputs so any future regression
that changed one case (e.g. made empty string panic) fires with a
focused message identifying the offending input.

Test result: 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 16s cold.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
